ops.body: Shipped across v0.16.0 → v0.17.1. format-core landed 3ebd66f (crate) + f31ef61 (wasm ABI) + 6c5ab42 (the wazero flip); corpus gates native + through-WASM in CI (155 pass / 2 locked >2^53-policy skips / 0 TOON drift). Release plumbing: v0.16.0 restored brew --HEAD (rustup-provisioned wasm32 toolchain in the formula head path — Homebrew rust ships no wasm32 std; validated end-to-end via a local tap) and resumed quill sign+notarize. POST-RELEASE DEFECT: v0.16.0 and v0.17.0 darwin binaries SIGKILL on ccx format — wazero's compiler engine mmaps RWX pages (plain mmap, not MAP_JIT), which the hardened runtime forbids without com.apple.security.cs.allow-unsigned-executable-memory; isolated by a 4-way codesign matrix. Fixed in v0.17.1 (entitlements restored to the sign block; release smoke now runs ccx format from the released tarball so this class can't ship again). Verified on the shipped v0.17.1: codesign chain + spctl accept + ccx format green; brew upgrade + plugin 0.17.1 cache pull confirmed. Downstream: cc-squash passes A/B consume the crate tag-pinned (v0.16.0; crate unchanged through v0.17.1) — contract recorded in cc-context AGENTS (1586d3f1).
